alreadyEarned.name: Google IT Support Professional Certificate
alreadyEarned.note: Many Per Scholas cohorts complete this alongside CompTIA A+. It's a well-recognized, legitimate entry-level IT credential on its own — list it even if CompTIA A+ isn't your path.
alreadyEarned.resumeBullet: Completed the Google IT Support Professional Certificate, covering troubleshooting, customer service, networking, operating systems, system administration, and security fundamentals.

alreadyEarned.name: Google AI Essentials
alreadyEarned.note: A shorter, newer credential covering practical AI tool use. Worth listing — a growing number of support and product roles now expect basic AI-tool fluency.
alreadyEarned.resumeBullet: Completed Google AI Essentials, covering practical use of AI tools for productivity, communication, and everyday problem-solving.

priceDisclaimer: Prices reflect vendor list prices as of mid-2026 and tend to rise over time — budget slightly higher, and watch for student, military, or nonprofit discounts plus employer tuition reimbursement once you're hired.

certifications.name: Microsoft 365 Fundamentals (MS-900)
certifications.bestFor: Quick credibility boost for help desk / M365-heavy roles
certifications.note: Cheapest option here, good ROI even before you've applied widely

certifications.name: Network+
certifications.note: Network+ holders average roughly $36/hr, but that blends all experience levels, not just entry-level

certifications.name: Security+
certifications.note: Often required (not just preferred) for defense-contractor-adjacent support roles

certifications.name: ITIL Foundation
certifications.costNote: roughly $300-$800+, no standalone voucher — self-study or bundled training
certifications.note: Lower urgency than MS-900 or Network+ at this stage

certifications.name: Azure Fundamentals (AZ-900)
certifications.note: Cheapest cloud credential, pairs well with MS-900

certifications.name: Endpoint Administrator (MD-102)
certifications.note: Bigger jump than MS-900 — save for after your first IT role

skillProjects.title: Windows troubleshooting notes
skillProjects.instructions: Create a one-page troubleshooting guide for five common problems: slow computer, no internet, printer not working, Outlook/email issue, password/login issue.
skillProjects.resumeBullet: Created troubleshooting documentation for common Windows, network, printer, and account-access issues using step-by-step support logic.

skillProjects.title: Ticketing practice
skillProjects.instructions: Use a free Trello, Notion, or spreadsheet board to simulate tickets with fields for issue, priority, troubleshooting steps, resolution, and escalation notes.
skillProjects.resumeBullet: Practiced ticket documentation, prioritization, and escalation workflows for common end-user support issues.

skillProjects.title: Microsoft 365 basics
skillProjects.instructions: Practice with Outlook, Teams, OneDrive, and basic Microsoft 365 admin concepts using free Microsoft Learn modules.
skillProjects.resumeBullet: Built familiarity with Microsoft 365 user-support workflows including Outlook, Teams, OneDrive, password/account issues, and user guidance.

skillProjects.title: Active Directory / user-management lab
skillProjects.instructions: If the computer can handle it, build a small home lab with VirtualBox/Hyper-V and Windows Server evaluation. Practice: create a user, reset a password, disable/enable an account, create a group, join a client machine to a domain.
skillProjects.resumeBullet: Built a small Active Directory lab to practice user creation, password resets, account lockouts, group management, and basic domain administration.

skillProjects.title: Basic networking lab
skillProjects.instructions: Document the home network: router/modem, Wi-Fi name, IP address, DNS, DHCP, speed test, basic troubleshooting steps.
skillProjects.resumeBullet: Documented and troubleshot a home network using basic IP, DNS, DHCP, Wi-Fi, and connectivity-testing concepts.
